title: Corrosylva
description: Industrial progression, research-driven technology, and spreading corruption. Unlock powerful machinery, survive polluted environments, and gate your path to the Nether.
body:
# Corrosylva

**Corrosylva** is a progression-focused Minecraft mod centered around industrial corruption, environmental hazards, gated research, and large-scale machinery. Advancement is controlled through research systems, protected structures, and powerful but risky tools.

---

## ✦ World & Materials

### 🌳 Corroded Wood
Corrosylva introduces a new wood type:

- **Corroded Wood**
  - Includes Corroded Planks and related blocks
  - Integrated into industrial and corrupted build sets

---

### ☣ Polluted & Corroded Blocks
Several blocks introduced by Corrosylva are **polluted** or **corroded** and can spread over time.

- Spreading behavior is visible when increasing `randomTickSpeed` (recommended ~100–400)
- High values may cause performance issues depending on simulation distance
- These blocks transform nearby compatible blocks into polluted or corroded variants

---

### ☣ Smog Storms
Around every 10-30 minutes or more, a **smog storm** will begin. The player will begin to take damage every 10 seconds and be inflicted with mining fatigue, naseua and poison. You can hide in a shelter or underground to be safe from the storm. Storms last around 2-4 minutes. Particles will decrease visibility when you are outdoors.

## ✦ Research & Progression

### 📘 Innovation Codex
Players begin the game with the **Innovation Codex**, which governs research progression.

- Right-click in main hand to open a **research tree menu**
- Research nodes require:
  - Research Points
  - Occasionally additional item costs
- Completing research nodes may:
  - Unlock recipes
  - Reward items directly
- The system functions but may require patience due to minor instability

---

### 🧩 Research Shards
Research Points are obtained via **Research Shards**, found through:

- Looting certain structures
- Fishing
- Rare drops from creepers

Behavior:
- Shards vanish one at a time upon entering inventory
- Points are stored as a persistent, per-player stat
- Current total can be viewed in the Innovation Codex

---

### 📖 Item Info Book
Players also receive an **Item Info Book** at game start.

- Right-click to open
- Contains a single item slot
- Displays information about the inserted item

---

## ✦ Industrial Machinery

### ⛏ Tunneler
The **Tunneler** is a progression-locked mining machine designed for deep excavation.

#### Unlocking
- The crafting recipe is not known by default
- Requires obtaining a **Tunneler Manuscript**
- Manuscript is earned through Innovation Codex research

#### Power System
- Requires **1–4 Battery Blocks** placed adjacent (not above or below)
- Battery Blocks:
  - Charged by right-clicking with a **Power Core**
  - Maximum charge level: **10**

#### Operation
- Shift + right-click with an empty main hand to activate
- The Tunneler:
  - Moves downward
  - Mines:
    - 2 blocks outward horizontally
    - 1 block upward and downward
  - Destroys all blocks except:
    - Battery Blocks (which move with it)
    - Indestructible blocks (e.g., bedrock)

#### Energy Use
- Each downward movement consumes **1 battery charge**

#### Storage
- Broken blocks are stored internally
- Inventory size equals a chest
- Opening the inventory disables the Tunneler

---

## ✦ Security & Automation

### 🛡 Factory Defender
The **Factory Defender** protects industrial structures.

- Prevents specific blocks from being broken while active
- Protected blocks include:
  - Tunneler, Battery Blocks
  - Industrial lights, sensors, wiring
  - Redstone components
  - Industrial concrete, terracotta, alloy blocks
  - Corroded Planks and other structural elements
- The Factory Defender:
  - Cannot be obtained in survival
  - Drops **gold and redstone**, not itself
- Breaking the Factory Defender removes all protection

---

### 👁 Motion Sensor
- Detects entities entering its range
- Detection range extends **3 blocks** outward based on facing direction
- Emits a **redstone signal strength of 15** from its output side

---

### 🚨 Warning Light
- Emits a repeating **warning siren** when powered
- Intended for alarms and industrial alerts

---

### 💡 Industrial LED Lights
- Can be powered or toggled via right-click
- Adjacent lights automatically link together
- Turning one on or off affects all connected lights

---

## ✦ Environmental Hazards

### ☠ Scorvetch
- Applies the **Polluted** effect to entities
- Effect:
  - Deals periodic damage (similar to Wither)
  - Drains hunger faster
  - Penetrates armor

---

### 🌱 Toxic Sand
- May grow **Toxic Ferns**
- Toxic Ferns poison entities that walk into them

---

### 💣 Industry TNT
When ignited:

- Converts all blocks within **20 blocks** into polluted or corroded variants
- May ignite dirt blocks
- Applies **Industrial Fire** to all entities in range

**Industrial Fire:**
- Cannot be extinguished with water
- Requires **Purified Water**

---

### 🚰 Purified Water
- Created by smelting a water bottle in a furnace
- Used to extinguish Industrial Fire

---

## ✦ Weapons & Tools

### ⚔ Burst Sword
- Right-click to dash forward several blocks
- Has a cooldown between uses

### ⚡ Charged Burst Sword
- Upgraded version of the Burst Sword
- Damages entities passed through during a dash

---

### 🪓 Charged Alloy Axe
- While sneaking, breaking a log:
  - Destroys all connected logs and leaves (tree-felling behavior)
- Consumes additional durability proportional to blocks destroyed

---

## ✦ Dimension Progression

### 🔥 Nether Lock
- The Nether is inaccessible by default
- Players must obtain a **Nether Key**
- Right-clicking with the key unlocks Nether access

---

## 📦 Version
- **Current Version:** 1.0.0

---

## 🚧 Development Notes
Some systems—particularly research—may exhibit minor bugs but remain functional. Corrosylva is designed for continued expansion into deeper industrial gameplay, environmental corruption, and progression-based mechanics.

---

**Author:** Cassetu  
**Platform:** Minecraft Java Edition
